properties.remark: PUBLIC REPORTED MOSTLY NICKEL SIZE HAIL BUT SOME STONES WERE AS LARGE AS QUARTERS.

A TORNADO MOVED THROUGH SPARKS AND DESTROYED A CONCESSION STAND ALONG WITH REMOVING HALF OF A TIN ROOF FROM A STEEL BUILDING. THE TORNADO WAS RATED EF-0 AND WAS 20 YARDS WIDE AT A LENGTH OF A HALF MILE. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN BETWEEN 845 PM AND 850 PM CDT ON MAY 22ND. THIS IS A DELAYED REPORT FROM THE OFFICIAL STORM SURVEY.

A TORNADO CAUSING EF2 DAMAGE...WAS ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY 20 MINUTES...FROM 930 PM CDT TO 950 PM CDT. THE TORNADO CUT A 9.3 MILE PATH THROUGH KEYA PAHA COUNTY TO THE NORTHWEST OF SPRINGVIEW. NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED.

A TRAINED SPOTTER OBSERVED SEVERAL GUSTNADOES THAT DID DAMAGE AT THE WILSON FEED LOT. AT THE FEED LOT THE GUSTNADOES DESTROYED A RAISED, OPEN FACED STORAGE BUILDING. THE BUILDING FRAME SLAMMED INTO THE SOUTHWEST CORNER OF A LARGE GARAGE. DAMAGE ALSO INCLUDED DESTROYING AN EMPTY 1200 POUND STORAGE GRAIN BIN, MOVED A SCALE HOUSE BUILDING APPROXIMATELY TWO INCHES ON ITS FOUNDATION AND DAMAGED THE FOUNDATION TO A THIRD STORAGE BUILDING.

A BRIEF TOUCHDOWN OF A SMALL TORNADO WAS REPORTED BY THE PUBLIC. THE TORNADO DESTROYED THREE OUTBUILDINGS ON A FARMSTEAD NEAR GRANT NEBRASKA. RADAR INDICATIONS WERE THAT IT WAS ASSOCIATED WITH AN OUTFLOW BOUNDARY FROM A STRONG THUNDERSTORM.

SEWAGE PLANT IN LYNCH WAS OFFLINE FROM MIDNIGHT CDT THROUGH 200 PM CDT JULY 22ND...DUE TO TOO MUCH RAINFALL OVERNIGHT. AN AUXILIARY PUMP WAS UTILIZED TO PUMP THE EXCESS WATER AND THE SEWAGE PLANT IS BACK IN OPERATION. LEVEL OF PONCA CREEK NEAR LYNCH HAS DROPPED IN HALF.

A WET MICROBURST OCCURRED. VISIBILITY WAS LESS THAN 100 YARDS IN VERY HEAVY RAIN AND HIGH WINDS. LARGE DUMPSTERS WERE FLIPPED. NUMEROUS POWERLINES WERE DOWN IN THE CENTER OF TOWN. A SECTION OF THE ROOF OF THE 1ST INVESTMENT CENTER BLDG LIFTED OFF AND LANDED ON ANOTHER BLDG NEARBY. 2.5 INCHES OF RAIN WAS MEASURED. 5 HOMES WERE FLOODED AND SEVERAL ROADS IN TOWN WERE FLOODED CURB TO CURB.

THE WINDS DOWNED TREE BRANCHES AT LEAST 4 INCHES IN DIAMETER.
FOUR TO EIGHT INCH DIAMETER TREE BRANCHES WERE DOWNED BY THUNDERSTORM WINDS.

A GARAGE WAS LEVELED WITH THE ROOF BLOWN 100 YDS TO THE NORTH. THE SIDING WAS WRAPPED AROUND TREES. THE REST OF THE BUILDING WAS LAYING ON THE GROUND IN A PILE. A SCHOOL BUS DRIVER IN THE SAME AREA EXPERIENCED BLINDING HORIZONTAL RAIN.
THERE IS CURRENTLY NO POWER IN TOWN AND TREES ARE DOWN ALL OVER THE COMMUNITY. BUILDINGS WERE REPORTEDLY DESTROYED JUST NORTH OF TOWN.

THE THUNDERSTORM WINDS PRODUCED CONSIDERABLE DAMAGE IN AND NEAR AINSWORTH...INCLUDING THE DESTRUCTION OF BOTH A 90 FT BY 30 FT HORSE BARN AND A SEPARATE STORAGE BARN. TREES AND POWER LINES WERE DOWNED...AND THE COMMUNITY WAS WITHOUT POWER FOR ABOUT 2 HOURS.
WINDS ESTIMATED 50 TO 60 MPH.
STRONG WINDS AT 50 TO 60 MPH WRAPPED TIN AROUND A POST.
QUARTER TO SLIGHTLY BIGGER SIZE HAIL FELL BEGINNING AT 1230 AM CDT.
MARBLE TO QUARTER SIZED HAIL OCCURRED FROM 130 AM CDT THROUGH 145 AM CDT.
THIS IS A DELAYED REPORT.
HEAVY RAINFALL ALSO OCCURRING AT TIME OF THIS REPORT.
FEW GOLFBALL SIZE HAIL...MANY QUARTER SIZE HAIL REPORTED.
QUARTER TO GOLFBALL SIZE HAIL COVERING THE GROUND.
A FEW STONES TO THE SIZE OF GOLFBALLS. MOST STONES THE SIZE OF MARBLES.

PEOPLE SEEKING SHELTER IN A METAL QUONSET HUT DURING STORM SAID ENTIRE ROOF OF QUONSET HUT WAS LIFTED OFF STRUCTURE THEN SLAMMED BACK DOWN. REST OF STRUCTURE REMAINED INTACT. A 26 FOOT HORSE TRAILER WAS MOVED ONE FOOT.
THE WINDS WERE ACCOMPANIED BY ONE HALF INCH DIAMETER HAIL.
THE WINDS WERE ACCOMPANIED BY QUARTER SIZE HAIL.
STREETS AND HOMES ARE FLOODING IN BROKEN BOW...WHERE 2.23 INCHES OF RAIN HAS FALLEN SINCE 6 PM CDT. SOME ROADS IN RURAL CUSTER COUNTY ARE ALSO FLOODING.
WATER WAS REPORTED OVER CALLAWAY ROAD 1 MILE WEST OF BROKEN BOW.
WATER WAS REPORTED OVER HIGHWAY 21 SIX MILES SOUTH OF BROKEN BOW. WATER RECEDED LATER AFTER HEAVY RAINS SUBSIDED.
DAM IN PASTURE WASHED OUT AFTER HEAVY RAINFALL. WATER NEARBY WAS NEARLY 5 FEET IN DEPTH NEAR HOME AND COVERED WELL HANDLES.
DELAYED REPORT FROM DAMAGE WHICH OCCURRED ON SEPTEMBER 22ND. 2 CAR GARAGE AND SMALL OUTBUILDING DESTROYED BY DAMAGING WINDS.
ALL GRAVEL IN DRIVEWAY WASHED AWAY FROM VERY HEAVY RAINFALL. 3 SEPARATE RAIN GAGES ON SITE MEASURED 4.50 INCHES OF RAIN. REPORT IS DELAYED FROM SEPTEMBER 22ND.
OBSERVER REPORTED SMALL LIMBS DOWN FROM TREES.
THE PUBLIC STATED THAT A POWER LINE HAS BEEN KNOCKED DOWN CAUSING A PRAIRIE FIRE.
A STOP SIGN HAS BLOWN DOWN...ALONG WITH SEVERAL TREE BRANCHES. SIZE OF THE TREE BRANCHES IS UNKNOWN AT THIS TIME.
TREE DAMAGE AND NUMEROUS POWERLINES DOWN IN MULLEN.
CHERRY COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTED VISIBILITY AT OR LESS THAN A QUARTER MILE IN VALENTINE.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED A ROOF RIPPED OFF A TRAILER AND LARGE TREE LIMBS DOWN IN TOWN.
PEA TO QUARTER SIZED HAIL OCCURRED FROM 1030 PM CDT THROUGH 1110 PM CDT...ALONG WITH VERY HEAVY RAIN.
